summary:
Prevna 13 is approved for use in children from birth through adolescence, specifically recommended for those aged 6 weeks to 5 years as well as older adolescents up to 17 years old. It helps prevent invasive pneumoccal diseases such as pneumonia or meningitis by providing targeted protection against common strains of Streptococcus pneumoniae.  

Note that this summary does not include any information about adult patients beyond what was provided in the original text.

The correct answer should only contain two sentences focusing solely on clinically significant facts related directly to the child population based strictly upon evidence presented within the initial response content given above — no external assumptions allowed.

Final Summary:

Prevna 13 is FDA-approved for preventing invasive pneumococcal disease in children aged 6 weeks through 17 years. Dosing varies depending on patient age group but remains effective at protecting younger and adolescent populations.
